title: Discussion: Teaching and Learning
questions: What qualities make a good teacher?
Do you think teaching methods have changed over the years?
Is it better to learn from a person or from the internet?
Should teachers be strict or friendly with students?

title: Discussion: Friendship
questions: What makes a good friendship last?
Do you think online friendships are as valuable as in-person ones?
How has the way people make friends changed?
Is it important to have many friends or just a few close ones?

title: Discussion: Problem Solving
questions: Do you think problem-solving skills can be taught?
What types of problems do young people face today?
Is it better to solve problems alone or in a team?
How has technology changed the way we solve problems?

title: Discussion: Competition
questions: Is competition healthy for society?
Do you think children should be encouraged to compete?
What are the advantages and disadvantages of competition?
How do different cultures view competition?

title: Discussion: Helping Others
questions: Why do some people choose not to help others?
Should governments do more to help people in need?
Do you think volunteering should be compulsory for students?
How has the concept of charity changed in the modern world?

title: Discussion: Relaxation and Stress
questions: Why are people more stressed now compared to the past?
What are effective ways to deal with stress?
Do you think work-life balance is achievable?
Should companies provide relaxation spaces for employees?

title: Discussion: Travel and Tourism
questions: How has tourism affected local cultures?
Do you think travel broadens the mind?
What are the environmental impacts of tourism?
Should there be limits on the number of tourists visiting popular places?

title: Discussion: Technology in Daily Life
questions: Do you think people rely too much on technology?
What are the risks of depending on technology?
How will AI change our daily lives in the future?
Should children be limited in their use of technology?

title: Discussion: Reading and Knowledge
questions: Do you think people read less nowadays?
Are e-books better than physical books?
How important is reading for personal development?
Should schools focus more on encouraging reading?

title: Discussion: Lifelong Learning
questions: Is it important to keep learning throughout your life?
What motivates people to learn new skills?
Do you think the education system prepares people for real life?
How has the internet changed the way people learn?

title: Discussion: Goals and Ambition
questions: Do you think young people today are more ambitious?
Is it important to set goals in life?
What happens when people fail to achieve their goals?
How do cultural differences affect people's ambitions?

title: Discussion: Teamwork and Collaboration
questions: Is teamwork more important than individual effort?
What challenges do people face when working in teams?
How can leaders create effective teams?
Do you think remote teams can be as effective as in-person teams?

title: Discussion: Role Models
questions: What qualities make a person inspiring?
Do you think celebrities are good role models?
Is it important for children to have role models?
How have role models changed compared to the past?

title: Discussion: Sleep and Lifestyle
questions: Why do many young people stay up late?
How does lack of sleep affect productivity?
Do you think society puts too much pressure on people to be productive?
What are the long-term effects of poor sleeping habits?

title: Discussion: Decision Making
questions: What makes decision-making difficult for young people?
Do you think people make better decisions as they get older?
How much should parents influence their children's decisions?
Is it better to make quick decisions or think carefully?

title: Discussion: Success and Achievement
questions: How do you define success?
Do you think success means the same thing to everyone?
Is hard work more important than talent for achieving success?
How does society measure success differently across cultures?

title: Discussion: Work and Professionalism
questions: What qualities are most important in a professional?
Do you think job satisfaction is more important than salary?
How has the concept of a 'good job' changed over time?
Should people follow their passion or choose a stable career?

title: Discussion: Learning from Mistakes
questions: Why is it important to learn from mistakes?
Do you think society is too harsh on people who make mistakes?
How can failure lead to success?
Should schools teach children that it's okay to fail?
